# tsvsheet.examples

Example `.tsvt` spreadsheets for [tsvsheet](https://tsvsheet.com) — every language feature as a worked, runnable sheet. Each **Try** link opens the sheet in the [playground](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/), carried entirely in the URL fragment: nothing is uploaded. (Moved here from `tsvsheet.go/examples`.)

Each example is a single `.tsvt` spreadsheet — a TAB-separated grid whose cells are literal values or `=formulas` that address other cells in A1 notation (`B2`, `D2:D5`). Open one in the browser editor and edit any cell live (edits recompute through the same engine):

```sh
tsv serve grades.tsvt
# then open http://127.0.0.1:8080
```

Every example also renders straight to stdout — handy for a terminal demo or piping into other tools:

```sh
tsv render invoice.tsvt | column -t
```

A sheet with a `#!/usr/bin/env tsvsheet` shebang is directly **executable** (running `tsvsheet <file>` with no subcommand renders it); full-line comments are a first-line `#!` or any `#` (hash-space) line. And `parse` ⇄ `from-json` round-trips a sheet through a jq-friendly grid — `{"rows": [[…source…]]}`, plus a `"values"` computed grid with `--value`:

```sh
./celsius.tsvt                                   # executable
tsv parse invoice.tsvt | jq '.rows[1]'      # munge the grid
tsv parse invoice.tsvt | tsv from-json # exact round-trip
```

| Sheet | Demonstrates | Try |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [grades](grades.tsvt) | Per-row aggregates (`round(avg(B2:D2), 1)`) and a conditional text result (`if(E2 >= 70, "Pass", "Fail")`) that reads the average computed earlier in the same row. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [invoice](invoice.tsvt) | Per-row arithmetic (`Amount = Qty × Price`, `=B2*C2`) and a `Total` row summing the amount column over a range (`=sum(D2:D5)`). | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#s=SXRlbQlRdHkJUHJpY2UJQW1vdW50CldpZGdldAkzCTQuNTAJPUIyKkMyCkdhZGdldAkyCTEyLjAwCT1CMypDMwpHaXptbwk1CTIuMjUJPUI0KkM0ClNwcm9ja2V0CTEwCTAuOTkJPUI1KkM1ClRvdGFsCQkJPXN1bShEMjpENSkK) |
| [math](math.tsvt) | Error-value propagation: dividing by a zero denominator yields `#DIV/0!`, which flows through any expression that reads the cell. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#s=TnVtZXJhdG9yCURlbm9taW5hdG9yCVF1b3RpZW50CURvdWJsZWQKMTAJMgk9QTIvQjIJPTIqQTIKMjAJMAk9QTMvQjMJPTIqQTMKMTUJMwk9QTQvQjQJPTIqQTQKOAk0CT1BNS9CNQk9MipBNQo) |
| [squares](squares.tsvt) | The power operator (`=A2^2`, `=A2^3`) building square and cube columns, over a `Total` row that sums each column across a range (`=sum(B2:B6)`). | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#s=TglTcXVhcmUJQ3ViZQoxCT1BMl4yCT1BMl4zCjIJPUEzXjIJPUEzXjMKMwk9QTReMgk9QTReMwo0CT1BNV4yCT1BNV4zCjUJPUE2XjIJPUE2XjMKVG90YWwJPXN1bShCMjpCNikJPXN1bShDMjpDNikK) |
| [weather](weather.tsvt) | Per-row differences (`Range = High − Low`, `=B2-C2`) and a `Peak` summary row reducing a column with `max`/`min` (`=max(B2:B6)`, `=min(C2:C6)`). | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#s=RGF5CUhpZ2gJTG93CVJhbmdlCk1vbgk3Mgk1NQk9QjItQzIKVHVlCTc1CTU4CT1CMy1DMwpXZWQJNjgJNTIJPUI0LUM0ClRodQk4MAk2MQk9QjUtQzUKRnJpCTc3CTU5CT1CNi1DNgpQZWFrCT1tYXgoQjI6QjYpCT1taW4oQzI6QzYpCT1CNy1DNwo) |
| [inline-bars](inline-bars.tsvt) | **A chart you can `cat`** — `=rept("█", round(B2/max($B$2:$B$7)*20, 0))` scales a bar of block glyphs per row, so the computed grid _is_ a bar chart: readable in a terminal, a diff, or `column -t`. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [functions](functions.tsvt) | A reference sheet demonstrating every built-in function with a worked formula and computed result — math and trig, aggregate and statistics, text, logical and info, date and time, lookup, financial, JSON, URL and email parsing, crypto digests (`digest`/`verify` over the range's canonical serialization), and dynamic-array + timeseries showcases whose results spill down their columns. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#s=VFNWU0hFRVQgRlVOQ1RJT04gUkVGRVJFTkNFCWV2ZXJ5IGJ1aWx0LWluIHdpdGggYSB3b3JrZWQgZm9ybXVsYSAoY29sdW1uIEIgaXMgdGhlIGNvbXB1dGVkIHJlc3VsdCkKZGF0YTogbnVtYmVycyAgRDI6RDYJCQk1CUFwcGxlCTEwCQk9RDI-MwpkYXRhOiBsb29rdXAgICBFMjpGNQkJCTMJQmFuYW5hCTIwCQk9RDM-MwpkYXRhOiBjb25kICAgICBIMjpINiAgKERuPjMpCQkJOAlDaGVycnkJMzAJCT1END4zCgkJCTMJRGF0ZQk0MAkJPUQ1PjMKCQkJMQkJCQk9RDY---_-) |
| [isnow](isnow.tsvt) | A reference sheet for the `isnow(…)` clock predicate ([tsvsheet/isnow](https://github.com/tsvsheet/isnow)): 78 patterns across the whole pattern surface — symbol names, the shorthand ladder, field algebra (sets, spans, from-end, steps, BYSETPOS), intervals, pattern-level exclusions, and bounds/windows. Each cell is piped through `\| volatile()` so the column stays live against the clock — each pattern lending its own refresh cadence. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [cat](cat.tsvt) | A blinking ASCII cat: the eye glyph flips on `isnow("::+[4]")` and `volatile()` marks the cell live, so `tsv serve cat.tsvt` (or the playground) redraws it on the clock — the smallest end-to-end demo of clock-driven animation. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [tamper-evident](tamper-evident.tsvt) | **Tamper-evidence in plain text** — `=digest(A2:C4)` fingerprints the data range's canonical serialization and `=verify(sig, pub, A2:C4)` checks an Ed25519 signature over it (produced offline — signing is a CLI operation, never a formula): edit any signed cell and `Signed` flips to `FALSE`. The audit trail is the sheet itself. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [webhook-log](webhook-log.tsvt) | **JSON parsed by formulas** — each `Event` cell is a raw JSON document; `jsonget(A2, "type")` pulls fields out, `jsontype`/`jsonlen` inspect shape, and `jsonset(A4, "seen", TRUE)` rewrites the document with member order preserved. Pure parsing, no I/O. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [link-audit](link-audit.tsvt) | URL and email surgery: `urlhost`/`urlpath`/`urlquery` split links into columns (`ifna` covers a missing query parameter), `emailvalid`/`emailuser`/`emaildomain` audit addresses, and `urlencode`/`urldecode` round-trip RFC 3986 percent-encoding. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [moving-average](moving-average.tsvt) | Windowed series that spill: `=movingavg(B2:B8, 3)`, `=rollingmax(B2:B8, 3)`, and `=cumsum(B2:B8)` each compute a whole column from one cell — positions without a full window are an honest `#N/A`. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [order](order.tsvt) → [discount](discount.tsvt) | **Embedded sheets** — each `Line total` embeds the whole [discount](discount.tsvt) sheet as a function: `=sheet("discount.tsvt", C2, B2)` passes the unit price and quantity, and the cell's value is that sub-sheet's `=output(…)`. | run locally (embeds a sibling sheet) |
| [report](report.tsvt) → [bar-chart](bar-chart.tsvt) | **A chart written as a sheet** — [bar-chart](bar-chart.tsvt) is a spreadsheet-function taking four (label, value) pairs via `input(n)`, scaling bars with `=round(B2/max($B$2:$B$5)*100, 0)`, assembling SVG fragments with `concat(…)`, and `output(…)`-ing a complete SVG document; [report](report.tsvt) embeds it — `=sheet("bar-chart.tsvt", A2, B2, …)` — so `tsv render report.tsvt \| awk -F'\t' '$1=="Chart"{print $2}' > chart.svg` draws its quarters. The chart _is_ a sheet: parameterized, versioned, diffable. | run locally (embeds a sibling sheet) |
| [celsius](celsius.tsvt) | **An executable sheet** — a `#!/usr/bin/env tsvsheet` shebang and a `#` comment line, so `chmod +x celsius.tsvt && ./celsius.tsvt` computes and prints the table. Comment lines are skipped and don't occupy a row. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#s=IyEvdXNyL2Jpbi9lbnYgdHN2c2hlZXQKIyBDZWxzaXVzIC0-IEZhaHJlbmhlaXQuIE1ha2UgZXhlY3V0YWJsZSAoY2htb2QgK3gpIGFuZCBydW46IC4vY2Vsc2l1cy50c3Z0CkNlbHNpdXMJRmFocmVuaGVpdAowCT1BMio5LzUrMzIKMjAJPUEzKjkvNSszMgozNwk9QTQqOS81KzMyCjEwMAk9QTUqOS81KzMyCg) |
| [littles-law](littles-law.tsvt) | **Little's Law**, `L = λ×W`, run in both directions — arrival rate × time-in-system predicts how many items are inside (`=round(B2*C2/60, 1)`), and WIP ÷ throughput predicts lead time (`=round(B8/C8, 1)`) — with the law itself explained in the sheet's own `#` comment lines. Executable, and a good target for the trace command: `tsv explain D2 littles-law.tsvt`. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [pizza-value](pizza-value.tsvt) | Geometry meets lookup: `pi()` and `^` turn diameters into area-per-dollar, `index(…, match(max(…)))` names the best-value pizza, and `if` + `&` concatenation settles the old one-18-inch-versus-two-12-inch riddle. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#-IEIxMSwgIm9uZSAxOC1pbmNoIHdpbnMiLCAidHdvIDEyLWluY2ggd2luIikgJiAiIGJ5ICIgJiByb3VuZChhYnMoQjEwLUIxMSksIDEpICYgIiBzcSBpbiIK) |
| [latte-millionaire](latte-millionaire.tsvt) | The financial functions on the "latte factor": a `=7%` percent literal, absolute references (`$B$4`), `fv` compounding a skipped latte habit over decades, `fv` inverted to find the monthly saving that reaches $1M, and `sln` depreciating the espresso machine you'd buy instead. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#) |
| [caffeine](caffeine.tsvt) | **Pipe syntax** — every formula chains through `\|` instead of nesting calls: `=$B$1 * 0.5^(A6/$B$2) \| round(1)` decays a dose (the pipe binds loosest, so the whole expression is piped), `ifs` grades the result, and `=$B$1/$B$3 \| log(2) \| product($B$2) \| round(1)` folds a left-associative chain into "hours until bedtime-safe". | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#--PSAkQiQzLCAiYWxlcnQiLCBUUlVFLCAicmVhZHkgZm9yIGJlZCIpCjYJPSRCJDEgKiAwLjVeKEE5LyRCJDIpIHwgcm91bmQoMSkJPWlmcyhCOSA-) |
| [password-lab](password-lab.tsvt) | **Pipe syntax** over text and logic: `=A2 \| len()`, `regexmatch` character-class probes whose `TRUE`/`FALSE` results are used as 1/0 in arithmetic to size the character pool, and an entropy chain `=G2 \| log(2) \| product(B2) \| round(0)` scored by `ifs`. | [playground →](https://tsvsheet.com/playground/#---PSA0MCwgIm9rYXkiLCBUUlVFLCAiaGFja2FibGUiKQoKU3Ryb25nZXN0CT1pbmRleChBMjpBNiwgbWF0Y2gobWF4KEgyOkg2KSwgSDI6SDYpKSAmICIg4oCUICIgJiBtYXgoSDI6SDYpICYgIiBiaXRzIgo) |

## Embedded sheets — a spreadsheet as a function

A cell can embed **an entire other sheet** and take its computed output as the cell's value, so a `.tsvt` becomes a reusable, parameterised function. Three builtins express it:

- **`output(expr)`** marks a cell as the sheet's single output (its value is `expr`).
- **`sheet(path, arg…)`** loads that sheet, computes it, and yields its `output` value; the extra arguments are passed in.
- **`input(n)`** reads the nth argument inside the embedded sheet.

So [discount.tsvt](discount.tsvt) reads `input(1)`/`input(2)`, computes a discounted total, and exposes it via `=output(C3)`; [order.tsvt](order.tsvt) embeds it per row. In the browser editor (`tsv serve order.tsvt`), selecting an embedding cell shows the nested sub-sheet inline. Referenced paths resolve within the sheet's own directory; a cross-sheet cycle is `#CIRC!`, an unresolved path `#REF!`. (Run `discount.tsvt` on its own and its `input(…)` cells are `#REF!` — it is meant to be embedded.)

## A note on the language

A `.tsvt` **is** the spreadsheet: there is no separate data file. Each cell is a literal value, or — when it begins with `=` — a formula over the Excel-faithful expression sublanguage: arithmetic (`+ - * /`), power (`^`), text concatenation (`&`), postfix percent (`%`), comparisons (yielding `TRUE`/`FALSE`), number / string / boolean / error-value literals, and builtins like `sum`, `avg`, `min`, `max`, `count`, `round`, `abs`, `len`, `concat`, `mod`, `if`. Formulas reference other cells by A1 address, exactly like a conventional spreadsheet; a reference off the grid resolves to `#REF!`, a cycle to `#CIRC!`, division by zero to `#DIV/0!`.

Worth knowing when you edit these: references are A1 (`B2`, `$B$2`, ranges `D2:D5`); `%` is postfix percent (`50%` = 0.5), so modulo is the `mod(a, b)` function.

Formulas can also chain through the **pipe operator** (§5.4 of the spec): `x | f(a)` is pure sugar for `f(x, a)`, left-associative and loosest-binding, so `=A1/B1 | log(2) | round(1)` is `round(log(A1/B1, 2), 1)`. The right-hand side must be a call with parentheses (`=A1 | len` is a syntax error). Array stages compose too — an inner array result is consumed by the next function exactly like a range, so `=A2:A10 | sort() | unique() | count()` counts the distinct values. The [caffeine](caffeine.tsvt) and [password-lab](password-lab.tsvt) sheets are written entirely in this style.

The full language is specified in [tsvsheet/tsvsheet](https://github.com/tsvsheet/tsvsheet).
